codeSystems.name: ResourceType
codeSystems.title: ResourceType
codeSystems.description: One of the resource types defined as part of this version of FHIR.

codeSystems.name: SearchEntryMode
codeSystems.title: SearchEntryMode
codeSystems.description: Why an entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

codeSystems.name: Icd10CM
codeSystems.title: International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM)
codeSystems.description: The International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM), describes the classification of morbidity and mortality information for statistical purposes and for the indexing of healthcare records by diseases. The ICD-10-CM codes can be used as the value of the Act.cd attribute.

codeSystems.name: RiskAdjustmentConditionCategoryType
codeSystems.title: Risk Adjustment Condition Category Type Codes
codeSystems.description: Code sytstem that indicates whether it is a suspected Condition Category (CC) gap, a historic Condition Category (CC) gap, or a net-new Condition Category (CC). Historic, suspected, and net-new are mutually exclusive.

codeSystems.name: CodingGapRemark
codeSystems.title: Coding Gap Remark Codes
codeSystems.description: Code sytstem that indicates coded remark for a coding gap.

codeSystems.name: RiskAdjustmentEvidenceStatus
codeSystems.title: Risk Adjustment Evidence Status Codes
codeSystems.description: Code System that indicates whether a coding gap is an open-gap, invalid-gap, closed-gap or pending

codeSystems.name: RiskAdjustmenthierarchicalStatus
codeSystems.title: Risk Adjustment Hierarchical Status Codes
codeSystems.description: Code system that indicates how hierarchies were applied to a Condition Category (CC), and if applied, whether the Condition Category (CC) is superseded.

codeSystems.name: RATemporaryCodes
codeSystems.title: RA Temporary Code System
codeSystems.description: Temporary code system defined in this implementation guide

codeSystems.name: RemarkReason
codeSystems.title: Remark Reason Codes
codeSystems.description: Reason codes for remark

codeSystems.name: SNOMED_CT
codeSystems.title: SNOMED CT (all versions)
codeSystems.description: SNOMED CT is the most comprehensive and precise clinical health terminology product in the world, owned and distributed around the world by The International Health Terminology Standards Development Organisation (IHTSDO).

codeSystems.name: CMSHCC
codeSystems.title: CMS Hierarchical Condition Categories
codeSystems.description:
The CMS-HCC model uses more than 9,000 ICD-10-CM codes, which are mapped to condition categories that predict costs well. The condition categories are based on diagnoses clinically related to one another and with similar predicted cost implications. Hierarchies are imposed on the condition categories to capture the most costly diagnoses. Hierarchy logic is imposed on certain condition categories to account for different hierarchical costs, thus, the term Hierarchical Condition Category, or HCC.

For more information, see https://www.cms.gov/Medicare/Health-Plans/MedicareAdvtgSpecRateStats/Risk-Adjustors.

The CMS HCCs are in the public domain and are free to use without restriction.

codeSystems.name: DefinitionTopic

codeSystems.name: MeasureImprovementNotation

codeSystems.name: MeasurePopulationType

codeSystems.name: ISO3166Part1
codeSystems.title: ISO 3166-1 Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions — Part 1: Country code
codeSystems.description: ISO 3166-1 establishes codes that represent the current names of countries, dependencies, and other areas of particular geopolitical interest, on the basis of country names obtained from the United Nations.

codeSystems.name: ISO3166Part2
codeSystems.title: ISO 3166-2 Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions — Part 2: Country subdivision code
codeSystems.description: ISO 3166-2 establishes a code that represents the names of the principal administrative divisions, or similar areas, of the countries and entities included in ISO 3166-1.
